content: anyway yeah you can send me what you want to say and I can check it, but tbh light communicates in english via MTL so I don't think he'd be upset if you used MTL to reach out to him (I can at least confirm that no meaning or confusion is being lost, at least)

Well Ace isn't responding to me, and I feel like Light has the information I need and Ace might not. I'll DM you my massive blocks of text and you'll see what I mean. Ultimately, as a capital A Anarchist I don't really care all that much about coming off as "somewhat disrespectful" as long as I'm being exceedingly polite, and what I'm saying is important. <:shrug:1003188954218123314>

<@183782216353054720> (replying here to not clog up the project channel)

D-Tide:

> 漆黑的潮水漫过警戒界限，轻微的波动亦昭示着足以吞噬掉以轻心者的险恶。 
```The Black Tide surges past the warning line; even the slightest ripples signal a perilous threat capable of devouring the careless.```
"漆黑的潮水" refers to the dark tide that comes from the Black Pool, which is a key term lorewise, so capitalizing it as a named term

> 黑潮漩涡翻涌，密境险象丛生，深沉的黑暗化为前行者的牢笼。
```The vortex of the Black Tide churns; perilous omens abound within the Domain as the profound darkness transforms into a cage for the wayfarers.```
"密境" is also a key lore term with regards to the Black Pool/D-Effect Zone, so capitalizing it as a named term
Also I honestly cannot make this line sound good despite spending way too much time on it so maybe someone with better writing skills than me can take a crack at it

> 旧日灾厄于此刻苏生，跨越现世的界碑，引燃堕落的汪洋。
```The ancient Calamity now awakens—crossing through the boundary into the present world, it sets ablaze the boundless sea of corruption.```

> 不可见的灾难复苏，物理世界的历史与存在正在坠入深渊。
```An unseen disaster revives; both the history and the very existence of this material world are sinking into the abyss.```
Again, can't make this sound good without changing the meaning substantially. I wish we had a skilled creative writer on the team

> 黑暗自深渊涌现，闯入者于紧攥心脏的恐惧中挣扎，在被黑暗吞没之前。
```From out of the abyss emerges darkness; intruders writhe amidst the heart-seizing terror before being devoured by the darkness.```
The first part of this can also be worded as "Darkness emerges from the abyss" for a more immediate/impactful feeling, but I went with a "From out of the abyss" structure because it seems a bit more literary/philosophical. Either would be fine, I think

Honestly the existing translation is mostly fine, feels like it might've already had a first-pass human edit

Phantasmal Dive:

> 「手」掀开夜的帷幕，悄悄来到这个世界。
```The "Hand" lifts the curtain of night, quietly arriving into this world.```
No issues with the existing translation, just made some minor formatting changes

> 此处埋葬着一所大学，千疮百孔，疲惫不堪
> 它曾为人类存续奉献了一点希望与勇气
> 至于结果如何，你现在都看到了
```
Here lies buried a university, scarred and shattered, drained of all strength
Once, it offered a faint glimmer of hope and courage towards the survival of humanity
As for the outcome—you can see it before you
```

> 坍塌的偶像注视着自己的子民。
> 看他们生存、繁衍、互相争斗，然后将自己彻底遗忘。
> 坍塌的偶像怀念着自己的子民。
```
The crumbling idol gazes upon its people.
It watches them survive, multiply, and fight; only to eventually forget.
The crumbling idol longs for its people.
```
No issues with the existing translation really, just a very minor wording change

> 「每把钥匙都有唯一的编号，只对应唯一的调查员。」
> 「因此，每把钥匙的陨落也对应着唯一的调查员之死。」
> 「真好啊。就连死亡都是独一无二的。」
```
"Each key has a unique number, corresponding only to a single investigator."
"Thus, the fall of each key signifies the death of a single investigator."
"How wonderful. Even their death is unique."
```
Also no issues with existing translation, minor wording changes

> 「它是唯一，它是全部，它是开启真相之门的希望。」
> 无数钥匙围成了一个圈。它们自天空中坠落，带给人们沉甸甸的希望。
> 也带来沉甸甸的坟墓。
```
"It is the One. It is the All. It is the hope which opens the gate of Truth."
Countless keys formed a circle. They fell from the heavens, bringing humanity a heavy hope.
They also brought heavy graves.
```
These aren't named terms but the phrasing makes it sound fairly significant and symbolic so I capitalized it to convey that feeling
Also I changed the tense as past-tense is implied more here than something current
Yay! Thank you! <:celeste_pray:1408324281624170566>

If I could, though, I'd like to give my own take on a couple lines.
Original:
```The unseen disaster resurfaces, dragging the history and existence of the physical world into the Abyss.```

Yours:
```An unseen disaster revives; both the history and the very existence of this material world are sinking into the abyss.```

"The" unseen disaster here being the Black Tide of Dissolution, I feel like it should be granted "The", or even "That".
With that context I feel like this line literally just describes what a wave of Dissolution does, and can maybe be played with a little? It's clear to me the narrative here is that of a literal wave, so "resurfaces" is almost there, but "revives" is probably the wrong direction. You chose not to use "Surge" in the next line, so I'll use that here.

Mine:
```That unseen disaster surges forth—swallowing the past and present of this material world into the depths of the abyss.```
I would also remove "the" from "heart-seizing terror". the heart-seizing terror is something the intruders are experiencing, not something that describes the darkness that emerged from the abyss

> As for the outcome, you can see it now

> As for the outcome—you can see it before you

I feel like this line should have a full stop. The other two in this poem shouldn't, but this one should and I can't prove it
also, "the outcome" of what exactly? the glimmer? the survival of humanity? and I feel like the translation of that second line entirely feels a little stilted as well.

```Here lies buried a university, shattered and scarred, drained of all strength
Once, it courageously offered a faint glimmer of hope for the survival of humanity
As for its fate—you can see it before you now.```

yeah I was split between an/the for the unseen disaster but whether or not said disaster is actually dissolution/the black tide is actually weirdly kind of vague - like obviously in context you think it is, but the word used for revive is like, very specific resurrection/revival/"comes back to life" and not resurfacing or reoccurring

so it kind of gives the feeling that the disaster could be some organic being and the unseen/invisible aspect of it might not be a named "The"...that said it's not really anything of importance so it's fine to use either an or the there so it doesn't matter either way. Up to you if you want to change it, dealer's choice basically <:casiah_cute:1408324282895040572> 

"surge" fits like multiple places but I only used it once across the lines because the lines show up in close proximity (since it's each difficult of dtide) and I didn't want repetition in wordchoice showing up when the original chinese word choice wasn't repeated, though again in this case the specific word used is 复苏 which does not actually fit surge at all because it's specifically something coming to life so using it to describe an inorganic existence doesn't fit well

I also really liked the present tense in the very last poem, and the very last line of it needs punctuality that is lost by treating it like a new sentence.

> "It is the One. It is the All. It is the All-One-o~~r-None~~
sorry brainrot
```"It is the One. It is the All. It is the hope which opens the gate of Truth."```
🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥

```Countless keys form a circle. They fall from the heavens, bringing humanity a heavy hope.
And heavy graves.```

so this is actually vague in that it can be read either way, the simple interpretation is that the intruders are experiencing fear, but it's a very literary sort of writing where it's possible to interpret it as the fear itself being an entity which is grasping their heart which would thus make the fear its own thing and not something of the intruders 

but again, it can be interpreted either way so up to you if you want to change it, not too important either/or
Surge could be something else for sure, it sounds like the English word we're looking for is "resurgence" but that's not gonna fit grammatically

the outcome is the ruins - you're standing before the ruined wreckage of the university, which once stood for the hope of humanity. what you see before you now is the result of said hope - i.e., nothing but broken wreckage and collapse

a full stop would fit but I didn't put one there because the original chinese didn't have one, but punctuation isn't necessarily 1-to-1 so it's fine to have a period at the end there

I feel like "anew" could definitely fit there somewhere. I like trying to hit that balance between "well if it was alive, they would have picked *these* words" and "well if it was an ocean wave, they would have picked *these* words"

pondering my orb

ok so rereading this on a more detailed level I see why the tense confused me

line 1: 「它是唯一，它是全部，它是开启真相之门的希望。」
- this is explicitly present tense

line 2: 无数钥匙围成了一个圈。它们自天空中坠落，带给人们沉甸甸的希望。
- so here the first part is actually explicitly past tense - the keys have already formed into a circle. The keys are already in a circle right now, they are not currently forming into a circle as we speak
- the fall part however is like, a term that kind of implies past tense since it generally means fallen, but the bringing is present tense so this is more of a current statement so it's actually a mixed tense thing

so to be accurate it should be:
```
Countless keys formed a circle. They fall from the heavens, bringing humanity a heavy hope.
```
for the last line, the abruptness and suddenness of just "And heavy graves." is imo much better than the original, but the original does very explicitly repeat the act of bringing
ahh, the translation of poetry.
so in terms of accurate translation it needs to be repeated in full, but this is one of those things where I think the liberty taken here is worth it
i.e., as a translator I would never, as an editor I would always
in the current role of a translator I take no responsibility for any changes
as an editor I would change it in a heartbeat and tell anyone complaining to sod off
I VOLUNTEER AS ~~TRIBUTE~~ EDITOR!

huh. actually, that's kind of a huge distinction that I hadn't thought about until just now.
no wonder I've been getting so much hesitation from Ace.
*I've been trying to be an editor in a room full of proofreaders.*
well editing is a spectrum
let me dig up ace's response actually because that was quite literally one of the first questions I had because it's an extremely, *extremely* important distinction

to which the answer was
> About Proofreading extent:
> 
> Proofreading for this project simply means editing the existing machine-translated text. Yes. 
> I know you're aiming for a high-quality, consistent output, and I truly appreciate that. To be blunt, we just don't have the resources or time right now to create a more detailed style guide or assign people to more specific roles. 
> I'm fully aware this might lead to some inconsistencies, and I've talked to Light about it. He's completely on board because we see any contribution as an improvement. 
> 
> While we'd love to have a more professional setup, we're making the best of what we have. 
> The good news is that we've been making more money lately, and Light said we'll soon be able to hire another person to help me manage and coordinate the volunteer project.

which basically means some leeway is allowed here and they're not setting a specific level so it's up to the individual (though per Ace's responses to you in later convos she doesn't want anything *too* aggressive)

I still really want to reach out to Light about some of these things, though it's the weekend now...
I'm just really hesitant about sending a message when it's possible some meaning could be lost in MTL since he doesn't speak English.
I have my whole writeup ready, it's a lot of text, and Cassius is a bit too busy to help me proofread a Chinese MTL of it. Would you be willing to help? 😅

anyway based on my completely subjective and extremely personal scale of the editing spectrum, I generally consider (on a scale of 1-10) anything 6 or 7 to be my personal allowable maximum, given a scale where:

1 = fixing typos and literally nothing else
2 = changing punctuation
3 = minor wording changes - "in" becomes "into", "an" becomes "the", maybe capitalization changes (basically anything I labeled "minor" up above I'd consider to fall under 3 or below)

4 = structural changes in presentation that don't affect meaning but do affect presentation - i.e., changing "Darkness emerges from the abyss" into "From out of the abyss emerges darkness"
6 = significant changes in wording that have the potential to change meaning but done elegantly should convey the exact same feeling - i.e., original "Here lies a university, riddled with scars and weary" becomes "Here lies buried a university, scarred and shattered, drained of all strength"

7 = pretty much a full rewrite but still using mostly the same words and meanings - the end result should clearly mean the same thing, but it'll read differently
8 = full rewrite but with totally different vocabulary and word choice (ideally this would be an "elevation", where dull text is raised to a more literary standard, but unfortunately most instances of this tend to change the tone/voice quite significantly which ultimately fails as a translation)
9+ = something which is pretty much an entirely new creation and completely fails to really communicate the intent of the original text whatsoever (you see this a lot in modern pop culture translations where people use very temporal slang like "rizz" or add unnecessary flash-in-the-pan cultural references that won't last for more than a couple of years)
(again this is my personal scale and not defined or applicable in any way elsewhere)

so I have some hesitation when it comes to contacting Light because I personally consider directly reaching out to authority to be somewhat disrespectful, but also because Light has been very clear that the community translation stuff is a separate project that he is not in charge of and is handled by Ace, so I'm not sure how well he'd take being contacted over it when the express implication here seems to be that Ace is the go-between

...but also I really, really, really, REALLY want some level of communication with the narrative team, not to drag them into translation stuff but solely as a way to clear up any unclear lore or worldbuilding aspects
